title: Bridge Essay: The empirical status of predictive coding and active inference.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dependent Arising** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.

**Phase context:** Predictive processing — free energy principle, active inference, Bayesian brain.

**The empirical status of predictive coding and active inference.** — Neurosci Biobehav Rev (2024). DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2023.105473. PMID: 38030100. Research on predictive processing models has focused largely on two specific algorithmic theories: Predictive Coding for perception and Active Inference for decision-making. While these interconnected theories possess broad explanatory potential, they have only recently begun to receive direct empirical evaluation. Here, we review recent studies of Predictive Coding and Active Inference with a focus on evaluating the degree to which they are empirically supported. For Predictive Coding, we find
